title: Repo conventions — hydroSense
text: squash-merge PRs. Merge merge commit (gh pr merge --merge) rebase (gh pr merge --rebase). Squashing collapses branch’s commits single new commit main, git branch --merged main can longer tell branch merged — every squash-merged branch shows “unmerged / ahead” piles stale clutter tedious risky audit later. Squash merging disabled repo settings; keep way. Delete branch soon PR merged (remote local). repo Automatically delete head branches enabled, merging PR removes remote branch; delete local copy (git branch -d <branch>). Don’t leave merged branches lying around. Merge commits rebase merges allowed; pick whichever gives cleaner history change.

what: How the mixture is combined (and the log-normal assumption)
title: Interpreting chronic msPAF outputs
text: msPAF combines substances two stages, following De Zwart & Posthuma (2005). Substances thought act mechanism combined within group concentration addition (CA); groups combined across independent action (IA). two stages make different assumptions underlying species sensitivity distributions, worth knowing . Independent action across groups shape-agnostic. IA combines groups msPAF = 1 − ∏(1 − PAF_i). needs component’s PAF, package reads full model-averaged SSD (six BCANZ distributions, weighted) — step holds whatever shape SSDs take. Concentration addition within group assumes log-normal. CA sums toxic units (TU_i = C_i / HC50_i) reads combined fraction single log-normal curve whose slope average component slopes. standard msPAF-CA method, exact component SSDs log-normal (log-logistic). best-fitting SSD substance shape, CA step approximates log-normal matched HC50 HC05. metals dominate leachate assessments approximation small — SSDs sit close log-normal — modelling choice, identity. future release add shape-faithful numerical CA (makes log-normal assumption) option; , substance’s SSD strongly non-log-normal, treat contribution within-group CA approximate.

title: Interpreting chronic msPAF outputs
text: Added Risk Approach (ARA) subtracts background “reference” concentration evaluating SSD, msPAF reflects increment local community already adapted . summary statistic used define background chosen summary argument prepare_reference(). package default "geom_mean". Useful reference dataset small (n < 20 per analyte) want propagate uncertainty visually downstream interpretation.

what: The state-space smoother in one paragraph
title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: residual latent impact state S modelled continuous-time AR(1) / Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process fitted grab-sample anchors Kalman filter + smoother (via KFAS package) daily grid. posterior mean smoother centre line, posterior covariance gives coherent draws — centre line credible band come one model. band pinches grabs (grab measurement width) balloons mid-gap; longer gaps balloon . Two parameters estimated per analyte anchor residuals: γ̂\hat\gamma — marginal (stationary) variance, well-identified moment: sample variance anchor S values. θ̂\hat\theta — mean-reversion rate (inverse correlation length), fitted 1-D maximum likelihood exact irregular-spacing OU likelihood, bounded correlation length stays within [0.5×,10×][0.5\times, 10\times] median anchor spacing. fallback ladder keeps sparse series well-behaved: analytes fewer ~8 anchors pin θ\theta long-correlation bound (≈ Brownian bridge); fewer two finite anchors, essentially constant series, “degenerate” add uncertainty. honesty note θ\theta. ~12–30 grabs typical routine monitoring, θ\theta (correlation length) weakly identified regardless estimator — likelihood θ\theta flat pinning autocorrelation decay needs many lags. smoother removes bias old method--moments estimate makes band coherent, manufacture information: band driven mainly γ̂\hat\gamma, sparse (“bridge-tier”) analytes degrade gracefully Brownian bridge. daily grid clipped per analyte analyte’s grab span: prediction produced first grab last (extrapolating management residual beyond data attempted), coverage can ragged across analytes.

title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: return = "summary" (default ndraws supplied) get one row per day three columns: mspaf — draws’ central tendency (central = "median" default, "mean"), summary posterior band always lies inside mspaf_lower — lower bound credible interval mspaf_upper — upper bound credible interval mspaf deterministic point estimate — see Two products: deterministic vs draws .
code: library(hydroSense)  demo   <- leachate_demo() target <- subset(demo, site_id == "downstream") ref_m  <- fit_reference_model(   subset(demo, site_id == "reference"),   hydro = demo_hydro() )  # 50 stochastic draws; return the 90% credible interval summary out <- mspaf_daily(   target,   reference_model     = ref_m,   interpolation       = "model",   ndraws              = 50L,   seed                = 42L,   return              = "summary",   interval            = 0.9,   require_temperature = FALSE )  head(out[, c("date", "mspaf", "mspaf_lower", "mspaf_upper")])

text: Pass grab_cv add lognormal grab-measurement error. scalar applies CV analytes; named vector sets analyte-specific CVs.

what: Widening the envelope, and hydrology-driven width
title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: ou_scale multiplies marginal variance γ\gamma (leaving θ\theta, hence correlation length, unchanged) — global knob inflate envelope default calibration feels narrow (infrequent grabs, highly variable analytes). kappa controls hydrology-modulated process variance: latent state allowed wander high-flow days, via qt=qbaseeκztq_t = q_{\text{base}}\, e^{\kappa z_t} ztz_t standardised short-window hydrology feature. makes band balloon faster across gaps span storms (stay tight across quiet ones). kappa = 0 recovers stationary envelope; default 0.5 makes ~95th-percentile flow day carry 3× base process variance. structural prior volatility, fitted quantity.
code: out_wide <- mspaf_daily(   target,   reference_model = ref_m,   interpolation   = "model",   ndraws          = 50L,   seed            = 42L,   ou_scale        = 2,              # double the marginal variance (global)   kappa           = 0.5,            # hydrology-modulated mid-gap widening   require_temperature = FALSE )

what: Checking calibration, and tuning ou_scale by tier
title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: bands right width? Don’t guess — measure, loo_anchor_coverage(). holds grab anchor (run consecutive anchors), refits smoother rest, checks often held-value falls inside nominal band. Run target model mspaf_daily() fits internally: Coverage reported per impact tier, two tiers usually behave differently: model analytes (enough detected anchors fit hydrology impact model) normally close nominal. bridge analytes (sparse fit, interpolated shared shape) tend -covered — bands narrow, smoother treats OU variance estimated handful anchors known. tier -covered, raise tier’s scale — ou_scale accepts vector named tier, can widen sparse bridge tier without inflating well-calibrated model tier: plain number (ou_scale = 2) still scales every analyte equally, . Coverage single site — especially sparse bridge tier — noisy, finite-sample estimate, pool across several sites committing scale rather -fitting one record.
code: tm  <- fit_target_model(target, reference_model = ref_m) cov <- loo_anchor_coverage(tm, interval = 0.9) cov #>   analyte   tier      n  coverage  mean_width #>   Cu        model    38     0.895        0.71 #>   Pb        bridge   10     0.500        1.62   # under-covered #>   (pooled)  <NA>    194     0.85          ... # Sweep the bridge scale until its coverage reaches the nominal interval … loo_anchor_coverage(tm, scale = 2)        # re-measure under a wider scale  # … then apply it in the pipeline, per tier: out <- mspaf_daily(   target,   reference_model = ref_m,   interpolation   = "model",   ndraws          = 200L,   ou_scale        = c(model = 1, bridge = 2)   # widen bridge only )

what: Speeding things up with parallel execution
title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: large ndraws multi-year grids, use parallel = TRUE future parallel plan. future.apply package must installed. Note parallel draws use independent RNG stream (L’Ecuyer-CMRG via future.apply), differ numerically sequential run seed, reproducible.
code: library(future) plan(multisession, workers = 4)  out_par <- mspaf_daily(   target,   reference_model = ref_m,   interpolation   = "model",   ndraws          = 200L,   seed            = 42L,   parallel        = TRUE,   require_temperature = FALSE )  plan(sequential)      # restore single-threaded execution

what: Interpreting the width of the envelope
title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: Narrow ribbon grab date: smoother pinned observed concentration (within grab measurement width). Remaining width comes GAM coefficient uncertainty (S1–S3) measurement error (S6–S7). Wide ribbon mid-gap: smoother posterior spread away anchors. width grows gap length, estimated γ\gamma (residual-state marginal variance), — kappa — hydrology gap, gap spanning storm balloons faster quiet one equal length. site infrequent, irregular sampling shows wider envelope one fortnightly grabs. Asymmetric ribbon: msPAF response concentration nonlinear (SSD-based) impact floored zero, ribbon skewed even though underlying concentration draws symmetric. direction skew data-dependent: symmetric concentration draws map SSD curvature, can make msPAF band wider centre line depending SSD site sits. Envelope cover centre line: never happen practice, ndraws small (e.g., 5) interval wide (e.g., 0.99), quantile estimates can noisy. Increase ndraws.

title: Daily msPAF with uncertainty quantification
text: mspaf_daily() gives one two distinct products, selected ndraws. answer different questions expected coincide. Deterministic (ndraws = NULL, default). single grabs-exact daily line: residual smoother pinned measured grab samples, curve threads reported lab values. Pro — fast, fully reproducible, best single-number estimate; ideal sanity check draws. Con — carries uncertainty, treats grabs exact (-fits noisy lab values). Draws (ndraws > 0). Monte-Carlo posterior propagates trend (GAM), -grab (OU bridge) — grab_cv set — grab measurement uncertainty. return = "summary" reports draws’ central tendency (central) plus credible band; return = "draws" returns raw per-draw paths. Pro — honest uncertainty quantification; summary mspaf band’s centre (always lies inside [mspaf_lower, mspaf_upper]). Con — central estimate now depends ndraws/seed, Jensen’s inequality bounded msPAF index generally sits deterministic line; adding ndraws therefore changes point estimate. natural workflow compute deterministic line (cheap) draws, overlay : deterministic best guess, band shows uncertainty around . deterministic strays well outside band, useful diagnostic, contradiction — two summarise different posteriors. Grab measurement error (grab_cv). per-sample lab uncertainties available, leave grab_cv = NULL (default): fabricated CV injects measurement spread real. real uncertainties land, draws use (widening band, pinching toward measurement width grab dates), deterministic line continues thread reported values.

title: Imputing missing and below-detection chemistry

what: The benchmark, and why "marginal" is the default
title: Imputing missing and below-detection chemistry
text: hold-benchmark real B.S01 data — masking ~10% detected routine-metal cells, pooled three mask seeds (57 held-cells), scored log₁₀ scale — gives: headline negative result borrowing: B.S01, letting sample’s observed metals condition missing ones help. "factor" method — principled low-rank latent-factor model exactly borrowing — worst performer. cell-level breakdown shows : loss concentrated sparse analytes (Pb, Cr), whose factor loadings unidentified, conditioning sample’s metals spurious loading yanks prediction far (worst Pb cell, ~30× low). well-measured metals merely equals "marginal". matches weak, ragged cross-metal correlation seen panel: little real signal borrow, borrowing noise actively hurts data thin. "marginal" — deliberately borrowing (per-analyte censored GAM) — therefore robust choice: best MAE, well-calibrated 90% coverage (0.91), tight informative intervals, needs neither brms cmdstanr, ~400× faster "rescor_mi". "rescor_mi"’s slim RMSE edge (0.25 vs 0.30) comes uninformative 100%-coverage intervals (width 1.29 vs 0.77) posterior saturates tree depth trips E-BFMI.

title: Imputing missing and below-detection chemistry
text: Per target analyte, fits log(conc) ~ s(PC1) + … + s(PCk) detected observations (mgcv GAM), predicts BDL/missing cell proper posterior predictive: GAM parameter uncertainty (β ~ N(coef, Vp), unconditional smoothing-parameter uncertainty included) plus residual-variance uncertainty (σ² drawn scaled-inverse-χ² posterior, making predictive Student-t), BDL cells’ draws truncated detection limit. assumptions: chemistry PCA carries signal. metal predicted water-quality context (major ions, pH, EC, nutrients, redox indicators) summarised PC scores — metals. panel strong, well-observed cross-metal coupling PCA already capture, "marginal" leaves table (use "factor"). Log-normal, smoothly-varying concentrations. spline mean assumes log concentration smooth function PC scores; residual Gaussian log scale. Detected-mean. GAM mean fit quantified values; heavily censored analyte mean can sit slightly high (small negative bias masked-detect benchmark, −0.14), fit never sees low tail. genuine BDL cells — truth really sub-limit — DL truncation correct conservatism.

title: Imputing missing and below-detection chemistry
text: "factor" — principled cross-analyte model. Use evidence genuine, reasonably-observed metal--metal coupling WQ PCA misses; beat "marginal" real signal borrow. weak/ragged panels mis-conditions sparse analytes (see ). "rescor_mi" / "cens" / "cens_factor" — brms methods, retained comparison continuity. "cens_factor" best-calibrated three; far slower "marginal" need Stan toolchain. much choice matter downstream? Usually little. Running full B.S01 daily-msPAF pipeline (mspaf_daily) different methods, everything else fixed, central estimates differ day--day median ~0.005 percentage points: panel site metals usually measured, imputation fills handful cells gap-uncertainty bracket dwarfs imputation effect. method choice matters width reported bands — "marginal"’s calibrated, tighter intervals honest default.
code: # Default — fast, calibrated, no Stan toolchain needed: model <- fit_imputation_model(monitoring_long)                       # "marginal"  # Opt in to cross-analyte borrowing (only worth it with genuine coupling): model <- fit_imputation_model(monitoring_long, impute_method = "factor")

title: Detecting leachate with the Leachate Mixing Fraction (LMF)
text: add_lmf() asks single question water sample: fraction sample’s chemistry can explained mixture local reference water landfill leachate? answer — leachate mixing fraction (LMF) — reported percentage: 0 means sample chemically indistinguishable local reference water, 100 means matches leachate end-member. source-detection index, deliberately separate toxicity: sample can carry clear leachate signature (high LMF) still well guideline values, vice versa. LMF built classic two-component end-member mixing model used catchment hydrochemistry (Christophersen & Hooper, 1992). Leachate-impacted freshwater treated blend two sources, major-ion signature used estimate blend ratio.

title: Detecting leachate with the Leachate Mixing Fraction (LMF)

Transforming species collapsed conserved totals — nitrogen species (NH3-N, NO3-N, NO2-N) total N, carbonate species total alkalinity — redox speciation shifts source sample break mixing assumption. Builds two end-members. reference end-member mean standard deviation ion local background water. leachate end-member anchored chloride (conservative tracer) expressed Cl-scaled ratios. Computes per-ion mixing fraction measured ion : fi=xi−RiLi−Ri f_i = \frac{x_i - R_i}{L_i - R_i}  xix_i sample, RiR_i reference, LiL_i leachate end-member. Combines ions one estimate inverse-variance weighted mean, ions measured precisely large leachate–reference gradient count . informativeness screen (reference scatter relative leachate–reference gap) admits sample enough high-information ions present. -weights disagreeing ions robust (Huber) reweighting pass, single ion behaving non-conservatively (sulfate reduction, extra source) drag estimate. χ² diagnostic flags overall ion disagreement.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense
text: ANZG freshwater toxicant guideline values derived specific index water-chemistry condition. ambient water chemistry differs condition, guideline — therefore effective toxicity — changes. hydroSense adjusts measured concentrations index-condition equivalents computing PAF values samples compared basis. adjustment : Cnorm=Cfield×EC10indexEC10fieldC_\text{norm} = C_\text{field} \times \frac{\text{EC10}_\text{index}}{\text{EC10}_\text{field}} normalised concentration enters SSD lookup. value Cnorm>CfieldC_\text{norm} > C_\text{field} means measured concentration toxic site conditions concentration index conditions; Cnorm<CfieldC_\text{norm} < C_\text{field} means less toxic. Normalisation formulas stored bundled analyte metadata CSV (inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv). can inspect override supplying CSV via analyte_metadata argument prepare_reference() add_mspaf().

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense
text: Source: ANZG (2021) ammonia freshwater DGV technical brief. Index condition: pH 7.0, temperature 20 °C. Required co-analytes: pH, temperature (°C). NH₃-N toxicity driven un-ionised fraction, increases sharply pH temperature. normalisation converts field total ammonia-N equivalent concentration index condition using ionisation equilibrium: fun(pH,T)=11+10pKa(T)−pHf_\text{un}(\text{pH}, T) = \frac{1}{1 + 10^{\,pK_a(T) - \text{pH}}} pKa(T)=0.09018+2729.92/(T+273.15)pK_a(T) = 0.09018 + 2729.92 / (T + 273.15). Water temperature must supplied analyte = "temperature" rows chemistry data frame. Use estimate_water_temp() air temperature records available.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense
text: Source: ANZG (2024) copper freshwater DGV draft technical brief, Figure 3. Index condition: DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analyte: DOC (mg/L). Cnorm=C×(0.5DOC)0.977C_\text{norm} = C \times \left(\frac{0.5}{\text{DOC}}\right)^{0.977} DOC clamped [0.5, 30] mg/L applying formula: Upper cap (30 mg/L): formula validated 30 mg/L DOC; use DGV 30 mg/L cases consider site-specific assessment. Lower cap (0.5 mg/L): index condition DOC, formula extrapolates unvalidated range; cap prevents unbounded upward correction.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense
text: Source: ANZG (2024) nickel freshwater DGV draft technical brief; invertebrate-trophic-level MLR Peters et al. (2021). Index condition: pH 7.5, Ca 6 mg/L, Mg 4 mg/L, DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analytes: pH, DOC, Ca, Mg (mg/L Ca Mg). Valid range: pH 6.0–8.5, Ca 2–70 mg/L, Mg 1.6–54 mg/L, DOC 0.5–20 mg/L. Peters et al. (2021) MLR invertebrates: log⁡e(EC10)=S+2.09log⁡e[DOC]+0.19log⁡e[Ca]+0.40log⁡e[Mg]−0.40pH−0.24log⁡e[DOC]⋅pH\log_e(\text{EC10}) = S + 2.09\log_e[\text{DOC}] + 0.19\log_e[\text{Ca}] + 0.40\log_e[\text{Mg}] - 0.40\,\text{pH} - 0.24\log_e[\text{DOC}]\cdot\text{pH} sensitivity coefficient SS cancels field--index ratio, : Cnorm=C×exp⁡(MLR(index)−MLR(field))C_\text{norm} = C \times \exp\!\Big(\text{MLR}(\text{index}) - \text{MLR}(\text{field})\Big) Important limitation: ANZG Ni DGVs derived using trophic-level-specific MLRs algae, aquatic plants, invertebrates fish applied species SSD. invertebrate MLR used appropriate single model macroinvertebrate-focused assessment, exactly reproduce published lookup-table DGVs, based full multi-trophic SSD re-derivation water chemistry combination. Users whose assessments span full range trophic levels may wish supply geometric-mean correction across four MLRs via custom analyte metadata CSV.

title: Analyte normalisation: bioavailability adjustments in hydroSense
text: Source: ANZG (2024) zinc freshwater DGV draft technical brief; Daphnia magna (Arthropoda / crustacean) MLR Gadd et al. (prep.). Index condition: pH 7.5, hardness 30 mg/L CaCO₃, DOC 0.5 mg/L. Required co-analytes: pH, hardness (mg/L CaCO₃), DOC (mg/L). Valid range: pH 6.2–8.3, hardness 20–440 mg/L CaCO₃, DOC 0.5–15 mg/L. D. magna MLR: log⁡e(EC10)=S−0.52pH+0.31log⁡e[hardness]−1.4log⁡e[DOC]+0.24log⁡e[DOC]⋅pH\log_e(\text{EC10}) = S - 0.52\,\text{pH} + 0.31\log_e[\text{hardness}] - 1.4\log_e[\text{DOC}] + 0.24\log_e[\text{DOC}]\cdot\text{pH} Note lower pH → higher Zn DGV (Zn less bioavailable low pH H⁺ competes Zn²⁺ biotic ligands). pH DOC clamped published valid range applying formula. Limitation — accuracy degrades pH 8: published Zn DGVs derived applying four trophic-level MLRs 31 species re-fitting SSD water chemistry combination. D. magna model alone gives reasonable approximation low neutral pH (within ~15% lookup-table DGV), diverges substantially high pH. pH 8.3, Daphnia-model-derived DGV approximately 2.4 µg/L versus published 1.0 µg/L, highly sensitive Chlorella sp. (PNG isolate, EC10 = 0.91 µg/L index) Raphidocelis subcapitata stronger negative pH coefficients dominate lower tail SSD alkaline pH. practical consequence: high-pH receiving waters (> 8.0), formula underestimate Zn toxicity treated caution. Users assessing alkaline sites consider supplying geometric-mean correction across four trophic-level MLRs via custom analyte metadata CSV.

title: hydroSense
text: hydroSense analyses water quality freshwater systems potentially impacted landfill leachate. turns routine monitoring chemistry ecologically interpretable, mixture-aware risk estimates, explicit handling -detection data local background conditions. package three pillars: Chemistry imputation. domain-agnostic Bayesian multivariate model (fit_imputation_model() / impute_chemistry()) imputes -detection missing analyte concentrations preserving cross-analyte correlation structure, can return full posterior draws. Analyte groups configured impute_group(); leachate_impute_groups() bundled leachate preset. Leachate detection (LMF). End-member mixing analysis (add_lmf()) estimates leachate-mixing fraction sample major-ion signature, distinguishing leachate influence natural variation. Multi-substance toxicity (msPAF). Species sensitivity distributions (ssd_paf()) feed multi-substance potentially-affected-fraction (add_mspaf()) combines toxicants concentration- response-addition subtracts local background via Added Risk Approach. background can static reference contemporaneous one (fit_reference_model()) predicts reference site shown sample’s moment hydrology season; season-blind companion (fit_target_model()) predicts impacted site grabs, mspaf_daily() can return continuous daily risk series. Toxicity grounded ANZECC/ANZG freshwater guideline datasets, bioavailability physicochemical normalisations (hardness, pH, DOC, temperature) applied every sample compared index-condition basis.

text: df Long-format dataframe. Required columns: sample_id, site_id, analyte, value, detected, datetime. leachate_data Optional long-format dataframe structure df providing leachate end-member chemistry. Required columns: sample_id, analyte, value, detected. supplied, samples used build leachate end-member regardless date regardless total-N content (total-N quality filter bypassed — supply curated data). Samples missing required LMF panel analytes silently dropped. NULL (default) uses standard leachate feature detection logic (requires dashboard infrastructure: feature_df(), data_df()). reference_data Optional long-format dataframe structure df providing reference site chemistry end-member calibration. Required columns: sample_id, analyte, value, detected. supplied, samples used build reference end-member regardless date; calibration window ignored. end-member applied globally features. NULL (default) uses standard per-feature matched reference site logic (requires dashboard infrastructure: feature_sfc(), data_df()). lmf_analyte_uuid UUID assigned LMF rows uuid.analyte column output. Set fixed UUID integrating data system tracks analytes UUID. Default NA_character_. calibration_window_years Number years calibration data use. Window centred input data's date range shifted backwards future data unavailable. Default 5. min_leachate_total_n Minimum total N (sum NH4-N + NO3-N + NO2-N) leachate sample qualify end-member calibration. Excludes non-representative samples (dilution events, treatment upsets). Total N used rather NH4-N alone aerated leachate features may nitrified NH4 NO3. Numeric units object; bare numeric requires min_leachate_total_n_units. Default NULL → 20 mg/L. min_leachate_total_n_units Character unit string min_leachate_total_n bare numeric, e.g. "mg/L". Ignored min_leachate_total_n units object NULL. rsd_default Default relative analytical uncertainty, applied sigma_meas = rsd_default * |x| floor rsd_default * |R|. Default 0.05 (5% RSD). min_ref_samples Minimum reference samples required calibration window. Features matched reference site fewer samples skipped silently. Default 10. min_leachate_samples Minimum valid leachate samples required end-member construction. function stops informative error met. Default 10. max_sigma_lsi Maximum permitted sigma_lmf percentage points (uncertainty mixing fraction estimate). Samples threshold return NA reason code "insufficient_precision". Default 10 (.e., ±10 percentage points). max_chi2_per_df Maximum permitted chi-squared per degree freedom (computed original inverse-variance weights, robust reweighting). Retained diagnostic output cases. Default Inf (hard gate — robust reweighting handles outlier ions directly, making hard chi2 gate redundant samples). informativeness_threshold Threshold sigma_R / |L - R| classifying ions high-information. Computed calibration data, per-sample. Lower values stringent. Default 0.20. min_high_info_ions Minimum number high-information ions must measured sample LMF computed. Samples threshold return NA reason code "insufficient_high_info_ions". Default 3L. robust_iterations Number Huber M-estimator reweighting passes. pass, ions whose studentized residuals exceed robust_threshold_k downweighted proportionally. Three iterations sufficient convergence practice. Set 0L disable robust reweighting entirely. Default 3L. robust_threshold_k Huber threshold standard-deviation units studentized residuals (r_i / sqrt(var_f_i)). Ions |sr_i| > k estimation weight reduced factor k / |sr_i|. Smaller values downweight aggressively; k = 2.5 close conventional Huber constant. Default 2.5. verbose TRUE, prints ion informativeness table via cli::cli_inform() showing ion's score high/low classification. Useful tuning informativeness_threshold. Default FALSE.

title: Compute msPAF for a single Concentration Addition group — compute_ca_group_mspaf

text: named list five elements: R              – [p × p] positive-definite correlation matrix (numeric matrix, dimnames). analytes        – analytes argument, preserved downstream use. tau2            – numeric scalar; estimated prior variance true Fisher-z correlation across pairs (EB shrinkage target; 0 pair reliable). n_co            – [p × p] integer matrix co-observation counts; n_co[, j] number dates finite S analyte j (diagonal = per-analyte finite-S count). nearpd_applied  – logical scalar; TRUE iff Matrix::nearPD() fallback fired.

title: Estimate a regularised cross-analyte correlation matrix from anchor residuals — .anchor_residual_cor
text: Pairwise complete observations (use = "pairwise.complete.obs"): ARA analyte panel ragged across sampling cadences (e.g. B.S01: NH3-N 121 grabs, metals 78–79, Al 7; complete-case 0 rows).  Pairwise estimation uses every available co-measured date pair, maximising information extracted sparse overlap. Reliability-weighted Fisher-z shrinkage: fixed ridge toward identity damps every pair equally regardless well estimated, pair n = 3 co-observed dates pair n = 100 shrunk fraction.  Instead, pairwise correlation r (n co-observed dates) Fisher-z transformed, z = atanh(r), approximate sampling variance v = 1 / (n - 3) (Fisher 1915).  empirical-Bayes (James–Stein-type) weight w = tau2 / (tau2 + v) shrinks z toward 0: z_shrunk = w * z, r_shrunk = tanh(z_shrunk).  Low-n pairs large v, hence small w, hence near-total shrinkage independence; well-sampled pairs small v, w near 1, survive close raw estimate.  Pairs n < 4 undefined/negative v set r_shrunk = 0 directly, killing spurious near-±1 correlations handful coincidentally aligned dates projection step .  tau2, prior variance true z across pairs, estimated reliability-weighted method moments reliable pairs (n >= 4, analytes non-degenerate): tau2 = max(0, weighted.mean(z^2 - v, w = n - 3)), following data-driven shrinkage-intensity idea Schäfer & Strimmer (2005) (covariance-shrinkage estimator adapted per-pair, count-aware weight rather single global intensity, ragged anchor panel gives every pair different effective sample size).  pair reliable, tau2 = 0, every weight 0, whole matrix collapses identity — conservative, independence assumption. Matrix::nearPD (Higham 2002, alternating projection algorithm): shrinking toward 0 z-space already removes spurious low-n -diagonals previously made pairwise matrix indefinite, safety net rarely fire.  retained fallback: applied minimum eigenvalue shrunk matrix <= 1e-8, case projects onto nearest (Frobenius norm) positive-definite correlation matrix; otherwise shrunk matrix used directly. Degenerate analytes (constant S < 2 finite observations): forced independent (identity row/col R_hat) rather propagating NA.

title: Antecedent Precipitation Index (API) — exact recursive linear reservoir — .compute_api
text: convergent infinite-horizon form classical Antecedent Precipitation Index (Kohler & Linsley 1951), whose theoretical basis Maillet (1905) exponential recession.  Unlike windowed sum truncation horizon: prior rainfall contributes exponentially decaying weight k^{dt} set actual day gap dt, irregular spacing handled exactly. Algorithm: lay rainfall onto complete daily grid (NA gap days contribute 0 input, store simply decays across ).  daily grid every step dt = 1, reservoir reduces first-order linear recursion S_t = k * S_{t-1} + P_t, evaluated C stats::filter() (method = "recursive").  exact reorganisation per-event form S_t = k^{dt} * S_{t-1} + P_t (k^{dt} just dt unit steps zero input events), none per-target re-summation old windowed form required.  Target dates index straight grid; falling first hydro day return 0 (empty reservoir).
code: S_t = k^{dt} * S_{t-1} + P_t,   k = exp(-1 / tau)

U_chol applied process innovations initial-state draws coupling coherent along entire trajectory. Independent observation noise: eps_std drawn independently per analyte measurement errors separate grab analyses uncorrelated even true concentrations co-move. Ragged grids: match(grid_a, U) slices union-grid field analyte's clipped grid.  Days analyte entry (outside grab span) field element — coupling days, correct.

title: Expand observed measurements into lognormal posterior-predictive draws — draw_measurement_error
text: Cells without reported error (NA zero error_col), BDL cells (detected = FALSE), already-drawn imputed cells pass unchanged. Lognormal parameterisation — geometric-mean convention: reported value \(v\) geometric mean (median) draw distribution. coefficient variation \(CV\): $$\sigma_{\log} = \sqrt{\log(1 + CV^2)},\quad       \mu = \log v,\quad       \text{draws} = \exp(\mathcal{N}(\mu,\, \sigma_{\log}^2))$$ arithmetic mean draws \(v \exp(\sigma_{\log}^2/2)\), exceeds \(v\) < 0.5 % 10 % RSD. Documented independence approximations: measurement error treated independent imputation error (cross-source index-pairing) independent across analytes within sample. Call order: impute_chemistry() → impute_coanalytes() → draw_measurement_error() → add_mspaf().  Imputation establishes draw count N; function reuses .

Apply lag air temperature regressing: water temperature day t predicted air temperature day t - lag_days. lag 1–3 days appropriate streams; 0 (default) appropriate ponds using aggregated data. site_id Character. Value use site_id column returned chemistry rows. Default NA_character_. seasonal One "auto" (default), "", "". Controls whether day--year seasonal term added air-temperature regression (see Model selection). "auto" fits air-air + season models keeps whichever lower AICc; "" forces air-model (legacy behaviour); "" forces seasonal model whenever eligible. seasonal_min_n Integer. Minimum number paired observations seasonal model even considered (default 8). seasonal model adds two parameters, small buffer needed AICc behave. seasonal_min_quarters Integer 1-4. Minimum number distinct calendar quarters training observations must span seasonal model considered (default 3). Calibration data confined one two seasons anchor annual cycle extrapolate badly. fallback Air--water relationship used paired observations fit regression (fewer 5 rows water_temp_obs, water_temp_obs = NULL). Accepted forms: "identity" — water = air (simplest default small streams). c(intercept, slope) — water = intercept + slope * air. function function(air) ... — arbitrary transformation. NULL (default) preserves existing behaviour: fewer 5 pairs hard error. select Antecedence window air-temperature regressor, days. regressor trailing select-day mean air temperature. "auto" (default) — select window leave-one-cross-validation (PRESS statistic, exact OLS) 1-SE rule: shortest window whose CV RMSE within one SE best chosen, avoid -fitting noise. Requires least auto_min_n pairs; falls back 1 (water_body_type default) pairs insufficient. positive integer — use fixed window (1 = daily air temp, equivalent original behaviour). auto_min_n Integer. Minimum number paired observations required select = "auto" window search attempted (default 20). threshold window falls back 1 (water_body_type). water_body_type Character. Physical type water body, used fallback window select = "auto" n_pairs < auto_min_n. One "stream_small" (1 d), "stream_large" (5 d), "estuary" (7 d), "pond" (10 d), "lake" (30 d), "reservoir" (60 d), "groundwater" (300 d). NULL (default) falls back window = 1. text: ANZG NH₃-N guideline values derived specific reference condition (pH 7.0, 20 °C total ammonia-N). normalisation formula adjusts measured NH₃-N conditions SSD lookup — since un-ionised fraction changes steeply temperature pH (roughly doubling 5 °C increase near 20 °C), using wrong temperature large effect normalised concentration. default value provided: must supply site-appropriate temperature estimates.

title: Estimate water temperature from air temperature measurements — estimate_water_temp
text: Window selection (antecedence timescale): water bodies thermal mass integrate air temperature longer periods. function searches log-spaced trailing windows 1 300 days, fitting simple OLS , selects leave-one-CV RMSE (PRESS statistic — closed form, refitting loop). 1-SE rule chooses shortest window within one standard error best, avoiding spurious preference long window barely beats shorter alternatives. window candidates number parameters, AICc distinguish ; CV required. Seasonal term (thermal hysteresis): water temperature lags air temperature seasonally — air temperature, water tends cooler spring warmer autumn. Adding first-harmonic day--year term — sin(2*pi*doy/365.25) cos(2*pi*doy/365.25) — lets regression represent . seasonal = "auto" window-regressor- window-regressor + harmonics models compared AICc; lower-AICc model used. title: Fit a temporal reference model for contemporaneous ARA background subtraction — fit_reference_model
text: Package invariant: reference target assumed share catchment.  hydrology series supplied (fetched automatically SILO) applies sites.  invariant hold near-field leachate assessments headwater reference paired downstream target sub-catchment. Three-tier resolver (evaluated per analyte × target date): Tier 1 — direct match. reference observation within ±match_window_days days whose event-API within match_hydro_tol (default: 0.5 × IQR reference API series).  gate rejects time-close hydrologically-mismatched grabs (e.g. dry-weather reference next wet-event target). Tier 2 — GAM prediction. per-analyte mgcv::gam s(doy, bs="cc") + s(hydro_short) + s(hydro_long).  reservoir recession constants tau_short, tau_long selected per analyte profiled AIC api_tau_bounds_short/api_tau_bounds_long ranges (continuous, tau_long >= 1.5*tau_short separation ΔAIC ≥ 2 adoption gate parsimonious default).  analyte falls back tier 3 best model higher AIC null (intercept-) model, fewer min_obs_model detected observations available. Tier 3 — static fallback. Geometric mean normalised reference observations (identical prepare_reference() summary = "geom_mean"). Hydrology input — supply exactly one : hydro: data frame columns date (Date) value (numeric), plus type column supply hydro_type = separately. Supported types: "rainfall" (daily mm; → API), "stage" (m; → antecedent mean), "discharge" (m³/s; → antecedent mean). latitude + longitude: hydro = NULL, SILO daily rainfall fetched automatically via get_silo_rainfall().  Requires weatherOz package valid SILO API key.

title: Fit a season-blind predictive model of the site impact — fit_target_model
text: season-blind. Site impacts driven management failure (leachate breach), calendar.  Heavy summer rain enables existing breach escape; cause one.  perfect-management year zero impact identical rainfall.  Day--year therefore confounded covariate impact excluded; hydrology enters modulator already-present impact expresses (first-flush mobilisation, sorption, antecedent memory). Model (per analyte). $$(t) = \beta \cdot f(\mathrm{hydro}_t) + S(t)$$ f(hydro) thin-plate GAM short- long-window antecedent indices (cyclic seasonal term), fitted beats intercept-null AIC least min_obs_model anchors available; otherwise beta = 0 analyte falls pure state-interpolation ("bridge") tier.  residual state S(t) interpolated observation anchors hydrology-weighted bridge pinches observed residual anchor leans toward hydrologically similar bracketing anchor . season-blind, hydrology-modulated variant WRTDS-Kalman (Zhang & Hirsch 2019); f(hydro) follows concentration–discharge theory (Godsey, Kirchner & Clow 2009).

what: hydroSense: Water Quality Analysis Tools for Leachate-Impacted Waters — hydroSense-package
title: hydroSense: Water Quality Analysis Tools for Leachate-Impacted Waters — hydroSense-package
text: Tools detecting assessing landfill-leachate impacts freshwater quality, organised around three capabilities. First, end-member mixing analysis estimates leachate-mixing fraction ('LMF') sample major-ion signature, distinguishing leachate influence natural variation. Second, Bayesian multivariate model imputes -detection missing analyte concentrations preserving cross-analyte correlation structure. Third, species-sensitivity- distribution ('SSD') based potentially-affected-fraction ('PAF') toxicity assessment, including multi-substance PAF ('msPAF') combines toxicants concentration response addition subtracts local background via Added Risk Approach. Toxicity derived ANZECC/ANZG freshwater guideline datasets, bioavailability physicochemical normalisations (hardness, pH, dissolved organic carbon temperature) applied samples compared common index-condition basis.

text: Diagnoses whether -grab uncertainty bands produced mspaf_daily() (interpolation = "model") calibrated data. modelled analyte holds grab anchor (run block consecutive anchors), refits OU/Kalman smoother rest, checks whether held-value lands inside nominal interval predictive band. result reported per analyte per impact tier, two tiers usually calibrated differently:

Leave-one-anchor-out coverage of the daily Kalman bands — loo_anchor_coverage
text: target_model fitted fit_target_model() object (class "target_model"). interval Nominal coverage test, (0, 1). Default 0.9. block Number consecutive anchors held per fold (default 1). block >= 2 probes mid-gap width rather just near-anchor pinch. scale OU process-variance scale used refitting, matching ou_scale argument mspaf_daily() (single number ). Sweep find value brings tier nominal coverage.

title: Leave-one-anchor-out coverage of the daily Kalman bands — loo_anchor_coverage
text: "model" analytes (enough detected anchors fit hydrology impact model) normally close nominal. "bridge" analytes (sparse fit, interpolated shared shape) tend -covered — bands narrow. Use measure, act mspaf_daily()'s tier-aware ou_scale: pooled bridge coverage interval, raise bridge tier's scale (e.g. ou_scale = c(model = 1, bridge = 2)) re-measure. See vignette("daily-uncertainty") full workflow. Coverage finite-sample estimate; anchors (especially bridge tier) noisy, prefer pooling across multiple sites choosing scale.

text: Orchestrates four steps chronic msPAF analysis usually chains hand: optionally fit Bayesian imputation model (fit_imputation_model()), fit reference/impact model (fit_reference_model()), compute daily multi-substance PAF (mspaf_daily()) target site, aggregate daily series chronic time-weighted msPAF (time_weighted_aggregate()). fitted imputation model threaded downstream chemistry steps -detection entirely-absent analytes filled toxicity calculation.

title: Run the full chronic msPAF pipeline end to end — mspaf_pipeline
text: target Long-format target-site chemistry (schema mspaf_daily()'s df): sample_id, site_id, datetime, analyte, value, detected. reference Long-format reference-site chemistry passed fit_reference_model(). hydro Optional hydrology series forwarded fit_reference_model(). impute Logical. Fit apply Bayesian imputation model? Default TRUE. FALSE, imputation model fitted NULL passed downstream (identical chaining functions hand without one). impute_on chemistry fit imputation model : "reference" (default, matching documented design) "target". required_vars Passed fit_imputation_model(). Default c("pH", "EC"). impute_groups Optional groups fit_imputation_model(); NULL (default) uses leachate_impute_groups(). impute_seed Optional integer seed imputation fit (forwarded fit_imputation_model()) stochastic fit reproducible. default "marginal" method's GAM fit deterministic; seed matters Stan-based methods ("factor" brms methods). reference_args, daily_args Named lists additional arguments forwarded fit_reference_model() mspaf_daily() respectively. include arguments orchestrator sets (reference/hydro/ imputation_model reference fit; df/reference_model/ imputation_model daily call). daily_args may override interpolation = "model" default. chronic = TRUE daily_args requests draws (ndraws), return = "draws" forced per-draw rows can propagated chronic summary; gap_uncertainty, interval central values daily_args (mspaf_daily()'s defaults "bracket", 0.9, "median") shape chronic envelope. chronic Logical. Aggregate daily series chronic time-weighted msPAF final step? Default TRUE. FALSE, daily mspaf_daily() result returned unchanged (model attributes ). focal_dates Focal dates chronic aggregation, passed time_weighted_aggregate(). NULL (default) derives sequence spanning daily date range via expand_focal_dates() (spacing focal_by). May also Date vector focal_date/site_id data frame. focal_by Spacing derived focal_dates focal_dates NULL; passed expand_focal_dates()'s (base::seq.Date() semantics): "day" (default), 7 (weekly), "week", "month", etc. Ignored focal_dates supplied. tau Exponential-decay parameter days chronic kernel, forwarded time_weighted_aggregate(). NULL (default) uses default 90 days. window Look-back window days chronic aggregation, forwarded time_weighted_aggregate(). NULL (default) uses default 365 days. chronic_summary Aggregation method chronic step, passed time_weighted_aggregate()'s summary. Default "arith_mean" (recommended aggregation bounded msPAF percentages).

title: Run the full chronic msPAF pipeline end to end — mspaf_pipeline

Set chronic = FALSE stop daily series reproduce previous (daily) return. Imputation default . individual functions treat imputation opt-(must fit model pass ). orchestrator flips default: impute = TRUE fits imputation model applies throughout. convenient free consequences — imputing -detection cells (replacing detection-limit values modelled sub-limit estimates) fabricating entirely-absent analytes generally moves msPAF result. title: Prepare reference chemistry for msPAF background subtraction — prepare_reference
text: reference_data Long-format chemistry data frame reference (background) site(s). schema input add_mspaf(): analyte, value, detected. Toxicant concentrations must µg/L normalisation; supply either via units.analyte column via conc_units argument. BDL (detected == FALSE) observations contribute 0 summary statistic. analyte_metadata Data frame analyte metadata, NULL load bundled inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. Accepts either data frame file path string. Must contain columns analyte, coanalytes_required, normalisation_formula. summary Summary statistic reference distribution. One "geom_mean" (default), "median", "arith_mean", "p80", "p90", "p95". bootstrap_ci Logical.  TRUE, compute 95% bootstrap CI reference summary statistic analyte (1,000 replicates default).  Adds ref_lower, ref_upper, n_boot_valid columns $ref_table.  Default FALSE. n_boot Number bootstrap replicates bootstrap_ci = TRUE. Default 1000L. conc_units Character. Unit string (e.g. "mg/L", "ug/L") applied uniformly SSD-eligible rows reference_data units.analyte column. Ignored reference_data carries units.analyte. Required data lacks units.analyte toxicant concentrations already µg/L. eps Small positive guard added inside log geometric-mean computation, handle BDL contributions 0.  Default 1e-9.

title: Prepare reference chemistry for msPAF background subtraction — prepare_reference
text: list class "prepared_reference" elements: $ref_table: tibble columns analyte, ref_norm (normalised summary concentration), n_obs (count observations contributing).  bootstrap_ci = TRUE, also ref_lower / ref_upper (95% CI) n_boot_valid (number bootstrap draws yielded finite summary; CIs built far fewer n_boot draws flagged warning). $dropped: character vector analytes excluded due reference observations. $summary: summary statistic used.

title: Prepare reference chemistry for msPAF background subtraction — prepare_reference
text: pure function — side effects internal cache. chronic pipeline, call computing chronic chemistry reference feature(s), pass object add_mspaf() every focal date.  per-sample pipeline, call raw reference chemistry. Summary statistic.  default "geom_mean" — geometric mean detected observations.  preferred fixed quantile : maximum-likelihood central tendency log-normal concentrations (aquatic concentration data typically distribute); uses observations rather single ranked point, robust small reference datasets; PICT-consistent: resident community adapted integrated typical exposure time, particular upper quantile. BDL observations contribute 0 geometric mean via \(\epsilon\)-shifted log: exp(mean(log(value + eps))).  summaries available: "median", "arith_mean", "p80", "p90", "p95".

what: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: Computes detection frequency (proportion samples detected == TRUE) per analyte returns names meet minimum threshold. Use impute_chemistry() drop analytes almost never detected — imputing analytes near-zero priors adds noise without ecological signal.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
code: prescreen_analytes(   df,   k = 0.05,   protect = NULL,   potency_keep = TRUE,   potency_frac = 1,   group_by_feature = FALSE,   conc_units = NULL,   analyte_metadata = NULL,   return = c("vector", "table") )

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: df Long-format chemistry data frame.  Required columns: sample_id (character), analyte (character), detected (logical). group_by_feature = TRUE, also requires site_id (character). k Minimum detection frequency (proportion, 0–1). Analytes n_detected / n_samples < k excluded unless protected. Default 0.05 (5 %). protect Optional character vector additional analyte names protect prescreen exclusion, top metadata-derived co-analytes.  Default NULL.  Pass required_vars downstream imputation step . potency_keep Logical. Enable potency escape hatch (keep frequency-failing analyte whose concentration reaches guideline value). Default TRUE. Set FALSE frequency-prescreen. potency_frac Numeric (>= 0). Fraction 95 % guideline value (dgv_95pct_ug_L) detected concentration must reach rescue analyte. Default 1 (must reach guideline). Lower (e.g. 0.5) precautionary. group_by_feature Logical. TRUE, detection frequency computed per site_id analyte included passes features (worst-case feature, pooled counts). FALSE (default), frequency pooled across samples. conc_units Character. Unit string (e.g. "mg/L", "ug/L") applied toxicant concentrations df units.analyte column. matters potency_keep = TRUE, potency threshold comparison needs values µg/L (matching dgv_95pct_ug_L). Ignored potency_keep = FALSE df already carries units.analyte column. analyte_metadata Data frame analyte metadata, NULL load bundled inst/extdata/anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. Used identify co-analytes must protected exclusion. return Either "vector" (default) return character vector included analyte names, "table" return tibble one row per analyte showing detection statistics inclusion flag.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: return = "vector": character vector passing analyte names. vector carries attribute "excluded" listing analytes pass threshold (non-protected ), callers can record dropped. return = "table": tibble columns analyte, n_samples, n_detected, detect_freq, limiting_site, protected (logical), potency_kept (logical; rescued potency escape hatch), included (logical).  group_by_feature = TRUE, n_samples / n_detected / detect_freq describe limiting site (worst-case feature drove inclusion decision) limiting_site names ; group_by_feature = FALSE, counts pooled across samples limiting_site NA.

title: Pre-screen analytes by detection frequency — prescreen_analytes
text: Analytes listed coanalytes_required bundled metadata (e.g. pH, DOC, Ca, Mg, hardness, temperature) automatically protected exclusion regardless detection frequency, needed chemistry normalisation add_mspaf().  Additional analytes can protected via protect argument (typical use: pass required_vars intend use fit_imputation_model() vars survive prescreen).  Protected analytes fall threshold reported separately caller aware. Potency escape hatch. Frequency alone can screen rare--potent toxicant (e.g. pesticide detected 2 % samples ecotoxicologically significant concentrations). potency_keep = TRUE (default), analyte fails frequency threshold still kept detected concentration reaches potency_frac times 95 %-species- protection guideline value (dgv_95pct_ug_L metadata). needs numeric value column units guideline (µg/L); analytes carry guideline value (toxicants) can rescued way, major ions analytes guideline unaffected.

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
text: df Long-format data frame.  Required columns: sample_id (character) — unique sample identifier site_id (character) — monitoring feature identifier datetime (Date POSIXct) — sample collection date/time analyte (character) — analyte / index name value (numeric) — concentration index value detected (logical) — whether value detected observation Optional: imputed (logical) — propagated n_imputed_in_window. focal_dates Either Date vector (applied features df) data frame columns focal_date (Date) site_id (character) specifying per-feature focal dates. tau Exponential-decay rate parameter.  Numeric units object; bare numeric requires tau_units.  Default NULL → 90 days.  effective half-life tau * log(2) ≈ 62 days default.  Choose match response timescale downstream biology (algae: days–weeks; macroinvertebrates: weeks–months; fish: months–years). tau_units Character unit string tau bare numeric, e.g. "d".  Ignored tau units object NULL. window Look-back window.  Numeric units object; bare numeric requires window_units.  Default NULL → 365 days. window_units Character unit string window bare numeric, e.g. "d".  Ignored window units object NULL. summary Aggregation method: "geom_mean" (default), "arith_mean", "p90".  See Choosing summary . anchor_outside_window Logical (default TRUE).  TRUE, recent sample window start included anchor provide duration weighting leading edge window. duration clipped start window_start contributes -window portion interval. eps Small positive guard added inside log geometric mean avoid log(0). Default 1e-9. return Output mode draw-carrier input (see summarise_draws()). "summary" (default) collapses posterior draws central estimate plus credible interval (value, value_lower, value_upper, n_draws); "draws" returns raw per-draw chronic rows (draw_id 1..N).  point (non-draw) input modes return byte-identical output interval columns.  Draws paired across time draw_id (index-pairing approximation assumes temporal independence; see OU/Kalman smoothing roadmap item). interval Width credible interval return = "summary". Default 0.90 (5th/95th percentile bounds). central Central-tendency statistic return = "summary": "median" (default) "mean".

title: Time-weighted chronic aggregation for any long-format value column — time_weighted_aggregate
code: # Chronic (time-weighted geometric-mean) chemistry for one downstream # analyte at two focal dates, from the bundled demo data. cu <- subset(leachate_demo(), site_id == "downstream" & analyte == "Cu") time_weighted_aggregate(   cu,   focal_dates = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-12-01")),   tau = 90, tau_units = "d", window = 365, window_units = "d",   summary = "geom_mean" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   focal_date site_id    sample_id     analyte value detected n_samples_in_window #>   <date>     <chr>      <chr>         <chr>   <dbl> <lgl>                  <int> #> 1 2024-06-01 downstream chronic_2024… Cu       7.56 TRUE                       3 #> 2 2024-12-01 downstream chronic_2024… Cu       7.50 TRUE                       6 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: n_imputed_in_window <int>

text: df Long-format dataframe. Required columns: analyte, value, units.analyte, valence.analyte, atomic_mass.analyte.

title: Convert analyte concentrations to milliequivalents per litre — to_meq
text: input df converted rows appended via bind_rows. Converted rows analyte suffixed "_" value meq/L. Rows lacking valence.analyte atomic_mass.analyte excluded conversion retained original rows.

title: Convert analyte concentrations to milliequivalents per litre — to_meq
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   analyte             = "Ca",   value               = 40.08,   # mg/L   units.analyte       = "mg/L",   valence.analyte     = 2,   atomic_mass.analyte = 40.08    # g/mol ) to_meq(df)   # appends a "Ca_" row of 2 meq/L (1 mmol/L x |valence| = 2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   analyte value units.analyte valence.analyte atomic_mass.analyte #>   <chr>   <dbl> <chr>                   <dbl>               <dbl> #> 1 Ca       40.1 mg/L                        2                40.1 #> 2 Ca_       2   mg/L                        2                40.1
